title: Epic: Implement and benchmark VRPTW optimization techniques from literature review

comments.author: m
comments.text:
## Epic 7j4 Reconciliation — VRPTW Literature-Review Techniques (2026-04-18)

Reconciled all 12 child issues against benchmark branches and main's current mod.rs.

### Merged to main (4)
- P1 / gk7 — SISRs string removal destroy operator (merged at d2e08d4; -0.38% on branch)
- P4 / bdu — O(1) move evaluation via RouteMeta (core merged at 1e99a65 for relocate; try_swap_star on xt4.10 also uses it)
- P5 / 1yj — Regret-3 insertion (merged at c0f987a)
- P6 / u1p — Granular/candidate-pruned neighborhoods for insertion operators (merged at c0f987a; SA-phase extension deferred)

### Merge candidates (benchmarked on branch, not yet merged) (3)
- P7 / giq — Or-opt-2/3 segment moves: -0.97% on branch
- P8 / pls — Ensemble destroy operator mixing: -0.68% on branch
- P10 / 79o — Instance-adaptive parameter tuning: -5.41% on branch (largest delta in epic, but baseline is stale pre-SISR/pre-regret-3; must re-benchmark against current HEAD before merging)

### Regressions / inconclusive (not merging) (5)
- P2 / rm3 — Time-warp penalties: +1.37% regression
- P3 / yj7 — SWAP* inter-route neighborhood (implemented on xt4.10/benchmark): +0.84% mean delta, within the measured 2-3% benchmark noise band; inconclusive
- P9 / 5u3 — Adaptive destroy size: +0.75% regression (closed earlier)
- P11 / 8pv — Multi-start diverse construction: +0.69% regression
- (xt4.8 SISR keep/revert investigation produced the sisr-revert-arm branch; keep arm shipped to main via d2e08d4)

### Still open (1)
- P12 / 9q0 — Elite archive with route recombination: NO benchmark run yet. Depended on 8pv which regressed, so motivation has weakened. Deprioritized.

### Key findings
1. The biggest wins came from machinery that sped up every iteration (RouteMeta O(1) evals, candidate pruning, SISR destroy) rather than from adding new neighborhoods.
2. New neighborhoods (SWAP*, time-warp, multi-start) at current per-instance budgets tend to steal iterations from cheaper operators without producing enough search progress to offset.
3. Benchmark noise (~2-3% run-to-run) dwarfs single-run deltas under +/- 1.5%. 79o's -5.41% is the only unambiguously strong candidate and even that is vs a stale baseline — re-benchmark required before merge.

### Recommended next actions
1. Re-benchmark 79o against current HEAD; if -1% holds, merge.
2. Merge giq and pls in sequence, re-benchmarking each time against the new HEAD.
3. Leave 9q0 open as a backlog item; revisit only if the portfolio needs more intensification.

title: SA-005: Require authenticated agent credentials on all agent-scoped routes
description: Issue a server-generated agent credential at registration and require it on heartbeat, state, iteration, experiment, hypothesis, and message routes. Reject calls where the credential does not match the claimed agent_id.
notes: Reopened after host-execution analysis. Completion requires authenticated access to agent-private /api/state (including best_algorithm_code and inspiration_code), not just write-route tokens.

comments.author: m
comments.text: Reopened: Host-protection requirement: best_algorithm_code returned by agent-private /api/state is part of the local code-execution path, so write-only auth is insufficient. Reopened to require authenticated agent-private state reads as well.

title: Epic: Execute the high-ROI VRPTW performance roadmap
description: Tracks the highest-ROI solver improvements from docs/research/applying-vrptw-findings-performance-roadmap-gpt54high.md. Scope: evolve the current one-file hybrid ALNS into a mini-HGS-style engine for the 24 Homberger 400-node instances by using the full time budget, replacing weak VRPTW-hostile operators, pruning neighborhoods, introducing O(1) route-segment move evaluation, and then adding SISR and SWAP*.

comments.author: m
comments.text:
Epic reconciliation (2026-04-18):

IMPLEMENTED (merged on main, closed):
- xt4.1: 27s time budget + cooling adjustment (cdc272f)
- xt4.2: 2-opt* tail swapping (0c0a3b1)
- xt4.3: ALNS bookkeeping: bitset, swap_remove, incremental dist (cdc272f)
- xt4.4: Candidate-list pruning + regret-3 upgrade (c0f987a)
- xt4.5: Regret-3 (included in xt4.4 commit c0f987a)
- xt4.6: O(1) relocate with RouteMeta (1e99a65)
- xt4.7: O(1) exchange/or-opt/2-opt* (already closed, e58cf64)
- xt4.8: SISR string removal destroy (already closed, d2e08d4)

IN-FLIGHT (worktree branches, not yet on main):
- xt4.9: Greedy-blink repair (deb8e7b on tig-swarm-demo-xt4.9/benchmark)
- xt4.10: SWAP* with candidate-pruned eval (851bf6c on worktree branch)

REMAINING: None — all 10 children are either implemented or in-flight.

Score progression: baseline ~7720 -> 7414 (xt4.1+xt4.3) -> 7453 (current main HEAD)

title: Use nearly the full 30-second solver budget
description: Extend the internal deadline in src/vehicle_routing/algorithm/mod.rs from the current ~4.5s to a safe ~27s budget while preserving frequent save_solution() checkpoints and feasible anytime behavior under scripts/benchmark.py timeout handling.
close_reason: Merged on main at cdc272f — extended solver budget to 27s with adjusted cooling rates, periodic save checkpoints

title: Add SWAP* with candidate-pruned evaluation
description: Implement SWAP* as an endgame intensification operator, using candidate pruning and the new move-evaluation machinery so the operator is computationally viable on 400-node instances.

title: Replace intra-route 2-opt with VRPTW-native 2-opt*
description: Remove segment-reversal-centric local search as a primary operator and replace it with inter-route 2-opt* tail swapping, which preserves directionality and is a better fit for tight time-window feasibility than classical intra-route reversal.
close_reason: Merged on main at 0c0a3b1 — replaced intra-route 2-opt with VRPTW-native 2-opt* tail swapping

title: Eliminate low-level ALNS bookkeeping overhead
description: Implement the cheap performance cleanups from the roadmap: replace linear membership checks in Shaw removal with a bitset or boolean map, avoid repeated rebuilds of remaining-index vectors in worst removal, and stop recomputing ALNS penalized_cost from scratch when route-distance deltas can be tracked incrementally.
close_reason: Merged on main at cdc272f — shaw_removal bitset, worst_removal swap_remove, incremental route-dist tracking

title: Prune insertion and local-search neighborhoods with candidate lists
description: Reuse the existing shaw_neighbors structure as a general candidate list so greedy insertion, regret insertion, and inter-route local search only examine geographically plausible neighbors and route targets instead of scanning all route-position pairs.
close_reason: Merged on main at c0f987a — candidate-list pruning for insertion operators using shaw_neighbors, plus regret-3 upgrade

title: Upgrade repair from regret-2 to regret-3
description: Strengthen the repair phase by moving from regret-2 to regret-3 insertion once candidate pruning is in place, aiming for better VRPTW repair quality without a large architectural change.
close_reason: Merged on main at c0f987a — regret-3 upgrade was implemented alongside candidate-list pruning in xt4.4

title: Introduce O(1) route-segment evaluation for relocate moves
description: Add route or segment metadata and concatenation-based evaluation helpers so relocate can be tested without cloning full routes and rescanning feasibility with is_feasible(). Include validation that the new evaluator matches current feasibility behavior.
close_reason: Merged on main at 1e99a65 — O(1) relocate move evaluation with RouteMeta scaffolding and concatenation-based feasibility

title: Extend O(1) move evaluation to exchange, Or-opt, and 2-opt*
description: After relocate is proven out, port the rest of the hot local-search operators to the concatenation-based evaluator so exchange, Or-opt, and 2-opt* avoid route-length feasibility rescans and share the same trusted move-evaluation machinery.

comments.author: m
comments.text: Benchmark complete. Score: 7377.29 (delta: -109.17, -1.46%). 24/24 feasible. Vehicles: 658. Commit: e58cf64

title: Add SISR destroy operator to ALNS
description: Implement Slack Induction by String Removals as an additional destroy operator inside the existing adaptive-weight ALNS framework, preserving the current operator pool while adding a stronger ruin phase for clustered and slack-sensitive instances.

title: Add greedy-blink repair operator to ALNS
description: Add a greedy-blink repair operator, suitable for pairing with SISR and for diversification inside the existing ALNS framework, rather than relying only on deterministic greedy and regret-based reinsertion.

comments.text: SWAP* with candidate-pruned evaluation implemented and benchmarked on xt4.10/benchmark (commit 851bf6c). n=2 mean 7516.13 vs baseline n=3 mean 7453.65, delta +62 (+0.84%) — INCONCLUSIVE (within ~2.6% run-to-run noise band). Per-arm runs: baseline [7354.79, 7548.75, 7457.42], SWAP* [7449.79, 7582.46]. 24/24 feasible on all runs. Operator is correct (unit tests pass, debug_asserts validate move eval). Not merging as-is; would need endgame-only scheduling or tighter candidate lists to steal less time from relocate/2-opt*.
